mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above 0.5 percentage points within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series exceed their 12-month moving averages, with a directional hit rate below 40% over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening corporate output and supply-side resilience, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a directional accuracy below 45% across 500 observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and rising corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals severe weakness in consumer demand and construction activity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in greater than 60% of months where HOUST drops below 1200.0, invalidating the recessionary signal.

mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate combined with stable unemployment indicates a successful soft landing policy mix, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 50% of cases when FEDFUNDS declines while UNRATE remains flat, with a strategy Sharpe ratio below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real gross domestic product growth confirms broad-based economic expansion, supporting corporate earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during mid-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during mid-cycle expansions, with a directional accuracy below 48% over a 10-year rolling sample.
